_comment: Single source of truth for the sshtool.app landing page feature list. Edited here; the web server fetches it from this repo (raw.githubusercontent) on a timer. The web server renders from this JSON server-side and falls back to a hardcoded list if it is missing. Keep prose factual, dense, no marketing fluff (see docs/APP_DESCRIPTION.md voice notes). No sensitive data: no real hostnames, IPs, emails, customer names.
tagline: Cross-platform SSH connection manager - terminal, port forwards, vault, all in one window.

scenes.title: Tree with folder inheritance
scenes.body: Credentials, jump hosts and SSH options cascade from the folder down. Live connections light up green and bubble a count to the parent folder.

scenes.title: Quick palette & live filter
scenes.body: Ctrl+K fuzzy-jumps to any host, tunnel or bookmark. Enter connects. Type > for app commands: lock the vault, open a workspace, check for updates. The sidebar filter narrows the tree for browse / right-click work without collapsing folders.

scenes.title: Tabs, splits, broadcast input
scenes.body: Multi-tab terminal with horizontal and vertical splits. Toggle broadcast to fan keystrokes out to multiple named groups at once - a session can sit in several groups in parallel. Themes, paste guard, search built in.

scenes.title: Live tcpdump panel
scenes.body: Stream packets straight into a side panel. Sudo handled via your saved password if the host needs it. Capped at 5000 packets server-side; brief output by default.

scenes.title: HTTP & SOAP probe
scenes.body: One-off REST/SOAP requests with a "Route via" dropdown of your active SOCKS5 forwards. Endpoints behind a VPN-style tunnel become reachable without leaving the app.

scenes.title: Overlay-network profiles
scenes.body: Reach hosts on a private network - a client's WireGuard, a Hetzner internal net, a NetBird or Tailscale overlay - by routing the SSH hop through a userspace tunnel. No system VPN, several up at once. WireGuard is built in; NetBird and Tailscale are one-click plugins. A folder-level Network setting sends every host under it through the same tunnel.

scenes.title: Give internet to an offline server
scenes.body: One click on a connected session raises a reverse HTTP proxy on the server and serves the proxying in-app - no squid, no external tooling. Paste the shown export block and the server's apt/curl/pip traffic flows out through your machine. DNS resolves on your side, so the box needs no working resolver.

scenes.title: Share a session with an LLM (MCP)
scenes.body: Attach an external LLM client (Claude Code, LM Studio, any MCP client) to a live session so it can read the terminal, pull logs, search and open your saved connections, and propose commands while you debug. Off by default, local-only bridge, per-session opt-in. Read-only commands auto-run; anything that changes state needs your approval. Terminal output is treated as untrusted data, and every action is logged in an activity panel.

scenes.title: Share a session to a browser
scenes.body: A colleague opens a link in any browser and watches your terminal live - or types into it, if you allow full control. You approve every guest and compare a short word-code to be sure it's them.

scenes.title: Dynamic inventory
scenes.body: Folders that auto-populate from Proxmox VE, Hetzner Cloud, DigitalOcean, Linode, Vultr, Scaleway, AWS EC2 and Ansible inventory files. Tags / labels filter what shows; stopped guests appear greyed with a "connect anyway" prompt. API tokens live in the vault.

scenes.title: VNC console, in-app
scenes.body: Open a Proxmox VM/LXC console or any VNC host as a tab, rendered by noVNC inside the app - no external client, no X server. Proxmox reuses your inventory API token; generic hosts dial direct or tunnel the RFB port through the connection's SSH session.

scenes.title: Runs on Android too
scenes.body: The same app - the same Go core, vault and SSH stack - runs on an Android phone (DeX-ready). Unlock with a fingerprint, a single-pane touch layout, an on-screen key bar for the keys a phone keyboard lacks, pinch-to-zoom font size, and a foreground service that keeps sessions alive in the background. opkssh login opens your browser; ssh-tool:// links open the app.

features.title: Tree with folder inheritance
features.body: Manage 300+ connections in nested folders. Credentials, jump hosts, and SSH options cascade down. Drag & drop, multi-select, Ctrl+K quick palette.

features.title: Dynamic inventory
features.body: Auto-populated folders that pull live from Proxmox VE, Hetzner Cloud, DigitalOcean, Linode, Vultr, Scaleway, AWS EC2 and Ansible files. Tag/label filters, hide-stopped, on-demand refresh, pin-one-host, convert-folder-to-static.

features.title: Encrypted vault
features.body: Passwords, keys and API tokens sealed with Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305. Machine-bound auto-unlock (DPAPI on Windows, machine-id on Linux), idle auto-lock, strength meter.

features.title: KeePass as a live backend
features.body: Reference a KeePass .kdbx entry directly - the password or private key is read from the file at connect time and never copied into ssh-tool. Local file or remote over WebDAV / SFTP, opened read-only. The database master is sealed in the vault, so one unlock opens both; remote copies are fetched fresh with a conditional request and fall back to the last cached copy (flagged stale) when the remote is down.

features.title: Tabs, splits, broadcast
features.body: Multi-tab terminal with horizontal/vertical splits, paste guard, themes, search. Broadcast input to multiple named groups in parallel - one session can broadcast to several groups at once.

features.title: Detach and redock windows
features.body: Tear off panes to separate windows, then snap them back. Tray icon, minimise-to-tray, close-to-tray.

features.title: Port forwards + isolated browser
features.body: Local, remote and dynamic SOCKS5 forwards. One click launches an isolated browser pinned to the SOCKS port. "Give internet" reverse-proxies an offline server's HTTP traffic out through your machine - no squid needed.

features.title: Debug with an LLM (MCP)
features.body: Share a live SSH session with an external LLM client (Claude Code, LM Studio, any MCP server) so it can read the terminal, pull logs, search and open your saved connections, and propose commands. Off by default and local-only: no session is reachable until you share it, read-only commands auto-run, and anything that changes state needs your approval. Terminal output is treated as untrusted data, and every action is recorded in an activity log.

features.title: Share a session to a browser
features.body: Let a colleague watch - or, with your approval, type into - a live session from a plain web browser, no ssh-tool on their side. You pick the tabs, the network interface, and read-only vs full control; every guest waits for your OK, and you confirm a short word-code with them so a leaked link is worthless. Encrypted over a self-signed certificate; splits, tab switches, and added tabs follow through live. LAN or over your own VPN - no cloud relay.

features.title: Userspace VPN profiles
features.body: Route a connection's first SSH hop through WireGuard, NetBird or Tailscale without a TUN adapter, admin rights, or system routes. Assign a profile to a folder and the whole tree rides the tunnel. Auto-mode dials direct when you're on-site, tunnel when remote. NetBird and Tailscale ship as optional plugins.

features.title: opkssh auth, native
features.body: OpenPubkey SSH certificates issued via OIDC, no external binary or filesystem dance. Cert and key live in the vault.

features.title: Live tcpdump + network insights
features.body: Streamed packet capture inline with per-protocol decode, sudo via saved credential when needed. A live analyzer flags routing and wrong-interface problems (0.0.0.0-bound replies from the wrong source IP, half-open TCP, ICMP unreachable, ARP off-subnet) and a one-click route check confirms the kernel's actual egress. Captures run in the background and survive detaching the tab to its own window.

features.title: HTTP / SOAP probe
features.body: One-off REST or SOAP requests with auto-detected route via an active SOCKS forward. Endpoints behind VPN become reachable in-app.

features.title: Workspaces & snippets
features.body: Named bundles of tabs restore your usual six terminals in one click - or let the app offer to reopen the last session's tabs at startup. Snippet palette fires reusable commands into the active session.

features.title: Batch exec
features.body: Run a one-off command across a multi-selection of hosts. Aggregate view with stdout, stderr and exit per host.

features.title: VNC console, in-app
features.body: Open a Proxmox VM, LXC, or node console - or any VNC host - as a tab, rendered by noVNC inside the app with no external client, no X server, no install. Proxmox guests reuse your inventory API token (vncproxy + vncwebsocket, self-signed clusters supported); node shells use a realm login; generic hosts dial the RFB port directly or tunnel it through the connection's SSH session. Fit scaling, Ctrl+Alt+Del; the optional VNC password lives in the vault.

features.title: Encrypted sync, your server
features.body: Sync the whole profile - connections, credentials, vault, icons, settings - between machines over any WebDAV server or SSH/SFTP server you control. Sealed with argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305 before upload; the server only ever sees ciphertext. SFTP auth reuses a vault credential or a key/password you type in, so a fresh machine can bootstrap. Auto-sync pushes on change; incoming changes apply live with no restart and, optionally, in the background while you work.

features.title: Session recording + player
features.body: Record a session's terminal output to standard asciicast v2 .cast files - output only, keystrokes never touch disk. Built-in player with seek, speed and idle-skip; files also play in asciinema or any web cast player.

features.title: Verified self-update
features.body: In-app updates stream with a progress bar and are sha256-checked against the release manifest before the binary is swapped. Leftovers from the previous version are cleaned up automatically.

features.title: Import from your old tool
features.body: Bring 600+ Devolutions RDM entries in one pass, your ~/.ssh/config with ProxyJump chains intact, PuTTY / KiTTY registry exports, or MobaXterm session files. All imports are additive and safe to re-run.

features.title: Android app
features.body: The same Go core, vault and SSH stack on an Android phone (arm64, DeX-ready). Biometric vault unlock backed by the device Keystore, a single-pane touch layout with system-back navigation, an on-screen key bar (Esc/Tab/Ctrl/arrows/^C^D...), pinch-to-zoom font size, and a foreground service that keeps SSH sessions alive when backgrounded. opkssh login opens the system browser; ssh-tool:// deep links open the app. Sideload the APK - no store account needed.
